summary: Iteration 3 (real per-source webhook ingestion) — full e2e PASS. Backend: 33/33 pytest tests pass (20 regression from iter2 + 13 new webhook tests covering CloudWatch ALARM→critical, Datadog error→high, PagerDuty urgency→high, Grafana/Alertmanager multi-alert, custom passthrough, 401 wrong/missing token, 404 missing source, 403 disabled source, /test endpoint auth + counter increment). Frontend: Settings page validated headlessly — webhook URLs render masked by default, eye toggle reveals/hides real token, copy works, PlayCircle 'test' button fires sample → toast 'Test successful · ingested 1 alert' → ingest_count increments from 0→1, 'last 5s ago' appears, Add Source flow creates a new row with its own 32-char ingest token and full webhook URL.

minor.issue: Default seeded sources (cloudwatch, datadog, pagerduty, grafana) have ingest_token of 26-28 hex chars instead of the spec'd 32-char hex. Tokens still authenticate fine — newly created sources via POST /api/sources DO produce 32-char hex tokens. Likely: previously-seeded data carried over from before iter3 had tokens lose leading zeros somewhere; on_startup backfill only fills missing/empty/null tokens, so existing short-but-non-null tokens are left as-is. Functional impact: zero. Cosmetic/spec impact only.

action_items: (Optional) Strengthen on_startup backfill: also rewrite tokens whose length != 32 OR are not strict hex, to bring legacy seeded sources up to 32-char spec. Code: change the $or filter on /app/backend/server.py:246 to also match {'$expr': {'$ne': [{'$strLenCP': '$ingest_token'}, 32]}}. Non-blocking — auth still works.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~948 lines (was ~760 in iter2) — well past the 700-line guideline. Webhook adapters (_norm_severity, _adapt_payload, SAMPLE_PAYLOADS) and the ingest/test routes should move to /app/backend/routes/sources.py + /app/backend/services/webhook_adapters.py.
POST /api/sources/{id}/ingest accepts arbitrary `payload: dict` — no size cap, no content-type enforcement. Add a max body size and reject non-application/json before prod.

POST /api/sources/{id}/ingest does not rate-limit per source — a misconfigured CloudWatch alarm could flood the alerts collection. Consider per-source rate limiting or dedup on (source, title, service) within a short window.

Adapter for cloudwatch only handles SNS Message-as-string in payload['Records'][0].Sns.Message; if AWS sends raw SNS w/ Message as already-parsed dict it falls back to top-level fields. Acceptable but document.

_adapt_payload swallows exceptions and returns []; ingest then returns 400 'Could not parse payload' which is correct, but the underlying error is only in logs. Consider returning the exception class in dev mode.
Pre-existing: server.py is monolithic, /api/sources mutations have no role-check (viewer can mutate), pickup unconditionally overwrites assignee, /auth/users is open to all roles.
seed_data_creation: Default sources seeded once on startup. New TEST_ sources created and cleaned up within tests. POST /api/seed re-seeds 10 deterministic alerts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v (~70s incl. 2 real Claude calls in chat tests). Webhook-only suite: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_webhook_ingest.py -v (~3s, no LLM). New frontend testids on Settings: webhook-url-{src_id}, reveal-token-{src_id}, copy-url-{src_id}, test-source-{src_id}. Webhook ingest is intentionally public (no JWT) — only per-source ingest_token via ?token= query OR X-Ingest-Token header. /test is JWT-protected and reuses _adapt_payload internally. Counters update on both /ingest and /test.
